data.title: Goldman Sachs Predicts Long-Term M&A Growth Despite Market Volatility
data.description: Wall Street is experiencing significant capital influx, which is expected to boost mergers and acquisitions (M&A) activity in the long term, despite current market volatility. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting insights from a leading dealmaker at Goldman Sachs. The executive emphasized that these substantial capital reserves could drive future dealmaking, even as fluctuating market conditions present challenges. The outlook suggests that while immediate M&A activity may face hurdles, the underlying financial strength in the market could support sustained growth in the sector over time.

data.title: UK Green Party Leader Criticizes Government's Business Policies
data.description: The leader of the UK's Green Party has expressed concerns over the government's approach to business regulation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the leader's remarks as he moderated the party's economic stance in preparation for the upcoming local elections in May. The Green Party aims to balance its environmental goals with economic growth, emphasizing the need for supportive policies rather than restrictive measures.

data.title: Denmark Initiates Coalition Talks as Frederiksen Eyes Third Term
data.description: Denmark is set to begin discussions to establish a ruling coalition, with caretaker Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen advancing towards securing a third term. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Frederiksen's efforts to consolidate her position in the government. The talks aim to form a stable coalition that can effectively govern the country, as Frederiksen seeks to extend her leadership. The outcome of these negotiations will be crucial in determining the political landscape of Denmark in the coming years.

data.title: Saudi Defense Minister Discusses Regional Security with Hungarian Counterpart Amid Iranian Attacks
data.description: Saudi Arabia's Defense Minister has engaged in a phone conversation with Hungary's Defense Minister to discuss the current regional developments. According to Jin10, the discussion took place against the backdrop of ongoing attacks by Iran on multiple regional countries. The ministers focused on the implications of these developments for regional and international security and stability. The conversation highlights the growing concerns over Iran's actions and their potential impact on the broader geopolitical landscape.

data.title: Ecopetrol's Leadership Decision Faces Internal Challenges
data.description: Ecopetrol's board is set to make a crucial decision regarding CEO Ricardo Roa on March 30. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the tension between political support and union opposition, which is creating significant challenges for Colombia's largest oil company. The decision comes amid internal divisions that could impact the company's future direction. As the board prepares to meet, the outcome will be closely watched by stakeholders concerned about the company's stability and strategic path.

data.title: Startale Group Secures $63 Million in Series A Funding to Expand Blockchain Products
data.description: Startale Group has successfully completed a $63 million Series A funding round, with a significant $50 million contribution from SBI Group following an initial $13 million investment led by Sony Innovation Fund in January. According to NS3.AI, the company specializes in developing blockchain products, including Strium for tokenized securities and real-world assets, the JPYSC and USDSC stablecoins, and the Startale app connected to Soneium. Startale plans to utilize the newly acquired capital to enhance its tokenized securities trading, broaden the application of stablecoins, and introduce tokenized stocks associated with Japanese equities within the year.

data.title: Solana Foundation Aims to Establish Solana as Core Infrastructure for Agentic Internet
data.description: The Solana Foundation has expressed its ambition to position Solana as a fundamental infrastructure for an agentic internet. According to NS3.AI, Vibhu Norby highlighted that agents have already facilitated approximately 15 million on-chain payments on the Solana network. Norby further suggested that stablecoins might become the primary payment method for computational resources, predicting that 95% to 99% of cryptocurrency transactions could eventually be initiated by large language models (LLMs).

data.title: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards: Air Defense System Downs U.S. F-18 Fighter Jet
data.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have announced that their air defense system successfully shot down a U.S. F-18 fighter jet. According to Jin10, this incident marks a significant escalation in tensions between Iran and the United States. The Revolutionary Guards did not provide further details on the location or circumstances of the incident. This development comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in the region, with both nations frequently at odds over various issues. The downing of the aircraft could potentially lead to increased military and diplomatic confrontations between the two countries.

data.title: JetBlue Explores Potential Sale to Competitor
data.description: JetBlue Airways is reportedly exploring the possibility of selling itself to a competitor. Bloomberg posted on X, citing a report by Semafor, that the airline is evaluating strategic options, including a potential sale. This move comes amid ongoing challenges in the aviation industry, as airlines navigate fluctuating demand and operational costs. JetBlue has not yet made any final decisions regarding the sale, and discussions are said to be in the preliminary stages. The company is considering various strategies to enhance its competitive position in the market. Further details on potential buyers or the timeline for any decision have not been disclosed.

data.title: U.S. Vice President Vance may travel to Pakistan this weekend for talks with Iran.
data.description: According to CNN, US Vice President Vance may travel to Pakistan this weekend for talks with Iran. This comes after an Iranian military source stated that Iran is continuously monitoring and understanding the movements and changes on its enemy's front lines. If the enemy attempts ground operations on Iranian islands or other territories, or increases Iran's defense costs through maritime activities in the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, Iran will respond by opening additional fronts. The source said the Bab el-Mandeb Strait is one of the world's most important strategic straits, and Iran has both the will and the capability to threaten it. He stated that if the US attempts to find a solution to the Strait of Hormuz issue through "foolish actions," it should be wary of allowing another strait to become its new predicament. The source also emphasized that Iran is fully prepared for further escalation of the situation. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Peter Schiff Warns of Potential Financial Crisis Amid Rising Import and Export Prices
data.description: Peter Schiff has issued a warning about a potential financial crisis in the United States, citing recent increases in import and export prices. According to NS3.AI, February saw import prices rise by 1.3% and export prices by 1.5%. Schiff suggests that the Federal Reserve should consider raising interest rates by several hundred basis points to address these issues. He also expressed concern that ongoing inflationary pressures could lead to a situation more severe than the 2008 financial crisis.

data.title: U.S. Airport Operations Disrupted Amid Funding Stalemate, TSA Reports
data.description: The U.S. Transportation Security Administration (TSA) has reported ongoing disruptions at major airports across the United States due to a funding impasse. According to Jin10, the situation has led to the resignation of 480 airport security personnel. The TSA is working to address the operational challenges as the funding deadlock continues to impact airport security staffing and efficiency.

data.title: U.S. EPA Temporarily Suspends Summer Gasoline Anti-Smog Rule to Ease Rising Oil Prices
data.description: The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has announced a temporary suspension of the summer gasoline anti-smog regulation in response to increasing oil prices. According to Jin10, this measure aims to alleviate the financial burden on consumers by allowing the sale of gasoline with higher volatility, which is typically restricted during the summer months to reduce smog. The decision reflects ongoing efforts to address economic pressures while balancing environmental considerations. The EPA's move is expected to provide some relief to the market, although it may raise concerns about air quality impacts during the suspension period.

data.title: EU Ambassador to US: EU Unlikely to Revert to Russian Gas Amid Middle East Crisis
data.description: The European Union is unlikely to return to relying on Russian natural gas despite the ongoing crisis in the Middle East, according to the EU Ambassador to the United States. According to Jin10, the ambassador emphasized that the EU has been actively diversifying its energy sources and reducing its dependency on Russian gas. This strategic shift comes in response to geopolitical tensions and the need for energy security. The ambassador's statement underscores the EU's commitment to maintaining its current energy policies and exploring alternative energy partnerships. The EU's stance reflects its broader strategy to ensure energy independence and stability in the face of global uncertainties.

data.title: The Trump administration has not received any official confirmation from Iran that it has rejected the proposal.
data.description: According to Axios reporter Barak Ravid, the Trump administration has not yet received any official confirmation that Iran has rejected the proposal. (Jinshi)

data.title: Grayscale Expresses Confidence in Emerging Cryptocurrencies
data.description: Grayscale has expressed its belief in the potential of emerging cryptocurrencies such as Solana (SOL), Sui (SUI), Tao (TAO), and Zcash (ZEC). Grayscale posted on X, highlighting its confidence in these digital assets, despite skepticism from some quarters over the past decade regarding Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH). The company continues to support a diverse range of cryptocurrencies, underscoring its commitment to the evolving digital asset landscape.

data.title: U.S. Officials: White House Plans Meeting with Iran in Pakistan to Address Crisis
data.description: Two officials from the Trump administration have indicated that the White House is working to organize a meeting in Pakistan this weekend to discuss crisis resolution with Iran. According to Jin10, the meeting aims to address ongoing tensions and explore potential solutions. The discussions are part of broader diplomatic efforts to stabilize the region and improve relations between the two countries. Further details on the agenda and participants have not been disclosed.

data.title: UK Junior Doctors Announce Six-Day Strike in England After Rejecting Government Proposal
data.description: Junior doctors in England have declared a six-day strike following the rejection of a government proposal, according to Jin10. The British Medical Association (BMA) announced the industrial action, highlighting ongoing disputes over pay and working conditions. The strike is set to impact hospitals across England, as junior doctors demand better compensation and improved working conditions. The BMA has been in negotiations with the government, but the latest offer was deemed insufficient by the union. This strike is part of a series of actions taken by healthcare professionals in the UK, reflecting broader dissatisfaction within the National Health Service (NHS). The government has expressed disappointment over the strike decision, urging further dialogue to resolve the issues. The strike is expected to cause significant disruptions in hospital services, affecting patient care and scheduled treatments.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin is under pressure in the $72,000 range, and indicators on multiple exchanges suggest weakening demand.
data.description: Bitcoin prices continue to be pressured below $72,000, with four on-chain data points indicating weakening market demand and limited short-term upside potential: 1. Glassnode's Accumulated Trend Score (ATS) is near zero, indicating that large holders are reducing or ceasing their BTC accumulation. This trend is similar to early 2025, when Bitcoin prices fell to $74,500, and small to medium-sized holders (less than 1,000 BTC) were also in a "distribution or inactivity" state. 2. Santiment data shows that Bitcoin whale activity is "historically low," with only 6,417 transactions exceeding $100,000 last week, and transactions exceeding $1 million dropping to 1,485, the lowest level since October 2024. Analysts say that smart money is cautiously observing due to the uncertainty surrounding the CLARITY Act and the prospect of war. 3. CryptoQuant's Network Activity Index has been declining continuously since August 2025, reflecting weakening overall on-chain demand. Bitcoin Vector's fundamental metrics also indicate weak network liquidity and growth, with the market described as "unsupported stability." Short-term gains rely more on fund flows, short covering, or external catalysts than on natural growth. 4. Bitcoin's hashrate has dropped sharply in recent weeks to 813 EH/s, a 22% decline from 1.2 ZH/s on March 5th. Rising energy costs and geopolitical conflicts have resulted in hashrate revenue per PH/s/day falling below $34, leaving most miners facing losses. Token Metrics analysts warn that if the difficulty drops by more than 5% within a week, miner exits will accelerate, potentially further increasing spot selling pressure. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: NYSE Options Trading Resumes Normal Operations After Technical Issues Resolved
data.description: The New York Stock Exchange's options trading division has resolved technical issues affecting trading, post-trade, and market data, according to Jin10. All systems are now functioning normally, ensuring smooth operations for traders and investors.
